title: Quadlet Volume Template Names
body:
### Feature request description

Currently #25902 handles using templated volumes [doc](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#referencing-other-template-quadlets), however, that is not very dynamic.
We would have to create a new volume template for config, data, cache, etc...

I would like to support `Volume=volume@volume-name.volume:/path`.

### Suggest potential solution

I want to create a single volume template:
```ini
[Unit]
Description=Volume for %I
Documentation=https://docs.podman.io/en/stable/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#volume-units-volume

[Volume]
# Troubleshoot generation with:
# /usr/lib/systemd/system-generators/podman-system-generator --user --dryrun
VolumeName=%i

[Service]

[Install]
# Rootful
WantedBy=multi-user.target
# Rootless
# WantedBy=default.target
```

Then in my `.container`, I can call it dynamically such as:
```ini
Volume=volume@%N-config.volume:/config
Volume=volume@%N-data.volume:/data
Volume=volume@%N-cache.volume:/cache
```

Using `%N` is another issue as currently Quadlet does not like that in my testing, but I believe that can be resolved in #29113.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Alternatively, I would need to define `volume-data@.volume`, `volume-config@.volume`, etc...
And any one-off volumes would need to be defined manually.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.
